title: [release-1.27] Bump runc up to 1.2.9 for CVE-2025-52881, CVE-2025-31133 and CVE-2025-52565
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind other

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

Backport PR #6484 & #6511

#### How to verify it

CI + Manual

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

The commits in this PR were created **with the assistance of AI**, from https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6538.  When reviewing please pay special attention to the following:

1. **Vendor directory consistency**:
   - Vendor directory was completely regenerated using `make vendor-in-container` after each `go.mod` change
   - Never manually edited, ensuring consistency with `go.mod` and `go.sum`
   - **Backport change**: Same process as source branch - vendor directory was regenerated after dependency updates to ensure consistency.

1. **All compilation verified**:
   - Project compiles successfully with `make` after every commit
   - All compilation errors encountered during backport were resolved and ammended to the commit.

1. **"Disable lint checking"**
   - This check fails on the branch even w/o any changes.
   - In CI the problem is difficult to diagnose as no output is provided,
     rather the process is simply killed.
   - **Backport change**: This commit was manually created, it does not exist on the
     source PRs.

1. **"Bump runc to v1.2.8 - CVE-2025-52881"** includes the lockfile API fix:
   - `internal/parse/parse.go`: Replaced `lockfile.Locked()` check with defer/recover pattern around `lockfile.Unlock()`
   - `run_common.go`: Same lockfile API fix applied
   - **Reason**: New lockfile API panics if unlocking an unlocked lock; the old `Locked()` method no longer exists

1. **Handle Cobra v1.5 -> v1.8 behavior changes**
   - Restore previous CLI behavior (Cobra 1.5) to match documentation.
   - `--userns-uid-map` and `--userns-gid-map` flags:
     - Left visible on the root command (buildah --help) to match `docs/buildah.1.md`
     - Hidden as local flags on build and from subcommands to prevent duplication
   - `--debug` and `-d` flags:
     - Hidden as local flags on the info command
   - `--compat-auth-file` flag:
     - Hidden as local flags on login and logout commands

1. **"Add a dummy 'runtime' that just dumps its config file"** applies PTY changes correctly:
   - Changes applied to `chroot/run_linux.go` (not `chroot/run_common.go` which doesn't exist in release-1.27)
   - Added import: `"github.com/containers/buildah/internal/pty"`
   - Replaced inline PTY code (lines 250-282) with: `ptyMasterFd, ptyFd, err := pty.GetPtyDescriptors()`
   - Removed unused `unsafe` import
   - Fixed type conversion: `os.NewFile(uintptr(ptyFd), "/dev/tty")` (converted `int` to `uintptr`)

1. Version bump is 1.27.7:
   - Created using `buildah_release 1.27.7` script.
   - Updated `define/types.go`, `CHANGELOG.md`, and `changelog.txt`

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *cevich* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [tomsweeneyredhat](https://github.com/tomsweeneyredhat) for approval. For more information see [the Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/release-1.27/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I'm loosing my :brain: here with `./hack/xref-helpmsgs-manpages` failures in the [Smoke Test](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6350039997480960?logs=validate_test#L27).  I've been backward and forward through the backported changes and don't see any that obviously add CLI options.  So they must be coming from someplace else?  What am I missing (and sorry if it should be obvious, my :brain: is really sore at this point)?

Edit: My clanker has identified the root cause as a Cobra v1.8 update + behavior change.  Code-updates are needed to fix this.

Backported:

- 91d83370b312e351c04f1162ff562081dec6d0f2 [[release-1.29] Bump x/tools to v0.26.0](https://github.com/nalind/buildah/commit/2a3f3ef05572c80ee2c961bfcca5328126e8a504)
- 288584dccd43715e0b550f133f689c96388f14b6 [[release-1.29] finish switching to moby/sys/capability](https://github.com/nalind/buildah/commit/70b7314c7ad05440a612c63a620dc958bde35003)

Note: I saw a few commits (6bf74008c64bda7261571e8365eca2307d0f417d and 56eadecf070051b28c12eb2ae50b86fd8787a0c3) I may consider bringing here in place of disabling the lint-checking, but it's a low priority ATM.
Note to me: CI first turned green in [fe9e14a](https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/fe9e14ac43a3d4c43d584980f78b58d5b8fdc553)

Force-push:

1. `--hard` reset onto 460b78697bbd3467894484df7c3d2e5a7d255b07 from https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6581
2. Updated "Bump runc to..." commit message, simplified it, updated links to RHEL 8.6 and 9.0 issues.
3. Regenerated the final "Bump Buildah to" commit to make changelogs current.
TONs of [quay.io flakes in CI](https://cirrus-ci.com/build/5399631850373120), I hit re-run on 5 or 6 of the integration tests.
@TomSweeneyRedHat @nalind PTAL I think this one is ready.

LGTM
As in #6538 you seem to be missing: https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6537/commits/3f365a394da258a3a97680ab4bae76f3dfc7e97d

Which, surprisingly, doesn't seem to be deadly to the tests.  We may need to reviist and add later if Podman goes south.
I'm going to backport it, it seems important.
force-push: Injected backport of "Partially work around containers/common" commit.

Note: I re-ran the two Integration test failures, both are networking flakes.

@nalind @Luap99 do you know if [this `TestGetSingleChroot` Unit test failure](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6087473815617536?logs=unit_test#L3696) is a flake?  The code seems to be relying heavily on asynchronous behavior and pipes, so maybe it's hitting some process, file or threading limitation.  The "vfs" flavor of unittests passed, so I'll re-run it assuming it is a flake.  Wanted to point it out though in case it's important.
Ref: Previously all-green (passing) tests prior to the changelog update: https://github.com/containers/buildah/runs/57678638956
